L1.area: RPC serialization and codec configuration layer

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where the protection mechanism used to restrict deserialization of potentially dangerous classes can be bypassed using a gadget chain composed entirely of JDK classes. This allows an attacker to achieve remote command execution without relying on any third-party components.

L3.area: RPC serialization and codec configuration layer
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where the protection mechanism used to restrict deserialization of potentially dangerous classes can be bypassed using a gadget chain composed entirely of JDK classes. This allows an attacker to achieve remote command execution without relying on any third-party components.

ground_truth.reason: The deserialization blacklist used by the SOFA Hessian protocol is insufficient, allowing a gadget chain composed entirely of JDK classes to bypass the protection and achieve remote command execution.
